further delay, let's get into the
executive summary. This week's key macro
question is, will Q2 earnings season be
a sell the news catalyst for AI stocks?
The short answer is the risk of this
likely transitory bearish outcome is
much higher than consensus currently
expects because of the rising
probability of negative revisions to AI
capex projections occurring concurrently
with the Fed that may opt the play
action pass i.e tighten cyclally to set
up the run i.e E ease structurally.
Refer to the monetary policy section of
our July 2026 macro scouting report for
more details. Terms of analytical
nuance, the stock market punished
revenue and earnings misses at one of
the most extreme rates in recent memory
throughout Q1 earning season. This
dynamic will likely be exacerbated by
price, meaning that the higher stocks
climb, the higher the tight rope for
companies to traverse to each subsequent
quarter. This is an increasingly acute
market risk because non-farm
productivity growth slowed to a well
below trend quarter over quarter saw to
of 0.3% in the most recent quarter
dragging the growth rates of Nipper
corporate profits the only indicator
that leads non-farm productivity late in
the business cycle and NIPA retained
earnings down with it. Recall that
non-farm productivity growth is among
the very best leading indicators of the
business cycle. It leads earnings
revisions by a full nine months late in
economic expansions which the current
levels of NEU and the output gap suggest
we are in. Even if you assume this is an
early cycle economic expansion
productivity growth leads earnings
revisions by four months. In terms of
the KISS model portfolio, no pivots.
We're still at 40% cash, 60% stocks, 0%
gold, and 0% Bitcoin. No upgrades or
downgrades in Dr. Mo today. So getting
to our fundamental uh research summary.
As always, we'll start with our active
themes. These are organized in
chronological order when we introduce
them to you the clients. So we'll start
with our sticky inflation theme. We
introduced that in January of 2022 uh
with the time rise in a one-year plus
from today. Recall that the consensus at
the time uh uh when we introduced the
theme was transitory inflation. Uh we
introduced our resilient US economy uh
uh in September of 2022 when the
consensus at the time was for a
recession. Uh that's got a time horizon
of one year plus from today. We
introduced our paradigm C aka run it hot
theme in April of last year when the
consensus at the time was that tariffs
were going to tank the US and global
economy. Uh that's got a time horizon of
one year plus from today. We introduced
our productivity boom theme in October
of last year alongside our jobless
recovery uh theme. Uh those have time
horizons of one year plus from today. We
introduced our cooling housing and labor
theme in December of last year with the
time horizon of one year plus from
today. And then we introduced our source
of funds theme in December of last year.
That's also got a time horizon one year
plus from today. source of funds being
that investors will uh on a long-term
basis use max 7 companies as a source of

beneficiaries the AI capex beneficiaries

profitability with the max 7 companies
uh so in terms of our general market
outlook we view the 12-month outlook as
bullish because the growth fiscal policy
and liquidity cycles are likely to
generally remain tailwinds throughout
and the inflation cycles is likely to
become a tailwind. The next few months,
however, may prove volatile because the
monetary policy cycle likely to become a
headwind amid positioning cycle
headwinds. In terms of the growth cycle,
our macro weather model currently views
the growth cycle as a modest near-term
headwind for asset markets. Our
resilient US economy theme is supported
by historically strong private sector
balance sheet dynamics. Our paradigm C
aka run hot theme is supported by
procyclical fiscal and regulatory easing
which are likely to perpetuate a
transitory boom in the economy
throughout 2026 and 2027. Our
productivity boom theme is supported by
capital deepening and AI diffusion. Our
jobless recovery theme is supported by
the cyclical underperformance of of
employment growth and the structural
downtrends in both labor supply and
labor demand. Our macro modest currently
views the inflation cycle neutral for
asset markets. Our sticky inflation
theme is supported by structural regime
change across a bevy of leading and
coincident indicators and the fact that
the Fed refuses to credibly pursue
disinflation. Our paradigm C aka run hot
theme is also supporting our staky
inflation theme. Our cooling housing and
labor theme is supported by our
productivity boom and jobless recovery
themes as well as lagged disinflation
and nationwide rents. Our macro weather
mono currently views the monetary policy
cycle and meaningful near-term tailwind
for asset markets. Our sticky inflation
and paradigm C aka run hot themes
suggest the Fed should tighten monetary
policy to regain some credibility on its
price stability mandate. Our research
suggests the Fed does not have to
tighten for economic reasons. Thus,
tightening would only be necessary to
limit financial stability risks,
stemming from a likely surprisingly
doubbish revision to the Fed's reaction
function upon the advent of Fed Sher
Walsh's task force findings later this
year, early next year. The Fed risk
upending the bond market if it pivots
directly to a surprisingly doubbish
reaction function amid sticky inflation
concerns. Our macro weather model
currently views the fiscal policy cycle
as a modest near-term tailwind for asset
markets. Our paradigm C aka run hot
theme is supported by proyclical fiscal
and regulatory easing. The
geopolitically driven supply of added
balance in the Treasury bond market, aka
paradigm A, has remained our core thesis
since we first published our investing
during the forthring regime analysis in
the summer of 2023. History confirms
there are only three options for
sovereigns to address unfavorable
disequilibrium in their bond markets.
Number one, cut the numerator, i.e.
debt, paradigm B. Number two, grow the
denominator GDP, i.e. paradigm C, our
current uh condition. Or number three,
print the difference, which is paradigm
D. We got a sprinkling of that as well.
Peril how every forturning since the
15th century has featured major
political realignment and total war aka
paradigm E. The US's violation of
Ferguson's law suggest the probability
of paradigm E occurring within the next
5 to 10 years is greater than 50%.
America model currently views the
liquidity cycle as a meaningful
near-term tailwind for asset markets.
The Fed's decision to counter repo
market stress with reserve management
purchases is likely an early signal of
the pending shift to paradigm D. The Fed
is the only institution in the world
with enough balance sheet capacity and
desire to counter the geopolitically
driven supply to add a balance in the
Treasury bond market. Thus, Fed
independence will likely continue to be
eroded akin to the prior forturn. Our
macro model currently views the
positioning cycle as a meaningful
near-term headwind for asset markets.
Our source of funds theme is supported
by the diffusion of AI throughout the
global economy. History confirms capex
bubbles tend to conclude with secular
bare markets. Thus, a secular bare
market is likely to follow the AI capex
bubble akin to the railroad, consumer
dual goods, and IT capex bubbles. This
thesis is supported by Ray Dallio's big
cycle framework, specifically the
breakdown of the domestic political
order and Peter Turin's elite overp
production framework. How Dio and
Turchin's independent frameworks point
to a rising probability of significant
redistri redistributive policies such as
wealth taxes and trust busting. And
finally, our quantitative versus
summary. These are organized in terms of
the time horizon of the signal. We'll
start with our short-term signals. Our
guarding model is not currently
generating any bearish or bullish
signals. Our probable range model
indicates no key macro market indicator
is currently overbought oversold. Our
short to medium-term signals uh have a
time horizon of two to three months. Our
macro weather models is currently
generating a bullish uh outlook for
stocks gold, Bitcoin, and commodities
and a bearish outlook for bonds of the
US dollar. Our macro weather model
currently signals a high probability of
sustaining a risk on market regime over
the short to medium term. And and
finally, our position model indicates
retail traders are neutral stocks and
professional traders are overweight
stocks. Our position model currently
signals low risk of a correction in risk
assets over the short to medium term.
And finally, our medium to long-term
signals. These tend to get resolved in a
3 to 12 plus month time horizon. Dr. Mo
is not upgrading or downgrading any
factors. Today, our global macro matrix
indicates reflation is the current
market regime. Reflation is a risk on
regime in which investors are generally
rewarded for increasing risk because
policy makers are supporting or unlikely
restraining nominal economic growth that
is perceived to be accelerating or
persistently higher than expectations.
The key portfolio construction
considerations and reflation are risk
assets tend not perform defensive
assets. High beta tends to perform low
beta. Cyclicals tend not perform
defensives. Growth tends to perform
value. New small mid caps t large caps.
International tends to not perform US.
Emerging markets tend not to perform
developed markets. Spread products tend
to not perform treasuries. Short rates
tend not to perform the belly which tend
not perform long rates. High yield tends
to perform investment grade. Endor
commodities cannot perform energy
commodities which tank commodities. And
then gold tends to perform foreign
currencies which tend out the dollar.
Our global liquidity model indicates
global liquidity is currently trending
higher. Key leading indicators of global
liquidity currently signaling modest
downtrend over the medium term. Our
group model indicates goldilocks which
is grow GDP growth accelerating and core
BC deflator or decelerating is the
consensus modal outcome for the US
economy over the medium term. And
finally, our position model indicates
investment advisors are overweight
stocks and underweight bonds and
underweight cash. A systematic funds are
neutral stocks. Market neutral hedge
funds overweight risk assets from a
gross exposure perspective. Risk asset
valuations are somewhat consistent with
major market peaks. Economic policy
uncertainty is not consistent with major
market peaks. Our position model
currently signals moderate risk of a
crash in risk assets over the short of
the medium to long term. And so for
